data.reasoningText:
The user wants to iterate quickly on a failing test without rebuilding each time. Let me look at their project to understand the setup and suggest the fastest workflow. They have a .NET test project with `dotnet watch test` being the likely best option.

Let me look at the files to understand the project structure.

data.content:
Use `dotnet watch test` — it watches for file changes and re-runs tests automatically without a full rebuild:

```bash
dotnet watch test --filter "ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException"
```

This will hot-reload on save. The fix itself is simple — add a negative-amount check in `ProcessPayment`:

```csharp
if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");
```

Want me to apply the fix and start the watch loop so you can see it pass?
